title: Ceropegia circes
taxonomy: Ceropegia circes is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Ceropegia circes is further classified as a genus of flowering plants within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. The subfamily Asclepiadoideae is known for containing plants with unique, tubular flowers. Ceropegia circes is native to parts of Africa, where it grows in dry, rocky areas.

It is believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating wounds and reducing fever. intro: Ceropegia circes, also known as the "String of Hearts" or "Circe's Ceropegia," is a flowering succulent plant species native to South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues, due to its potential calming and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia circes include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ceropegia circes is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
